160	// ─── StackValue ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161	
162	/// One slot on the Lua value stack.  Wraps a `LuaValue` and an optional
163	/// to-be-closed delta (for the `tbclist` mechanism).
164	///
165	/// C: `StackValue` in `lobject.h`.
166	/// types.tsv: `StackValue → StackValue { val: LuaValue, tbclist.delta: u16 }`
167	#[derive(Clone)]
168	pub struct StackValue {
169	    // C: StackValue.val — the payload TValue
170	    pub val: LuaValue,
171	    // C: StackValue.tbclist.delta — to-be-closed linked-list delta
172	    pub tbc_delta: u16,
173	}
174	
175	impl Default for StackValue {
176	    fn default() -> Self {
177	        StackValue {
178	            val: LuaValue::Nil,
179	            tbc_delta: 0,
180	        }
181	    }
182	}
183	
184	// ─── CallInfo ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
185	
186	/// Saved state for a Lua or C call frame.
187	///
188	/// C: `struct CallInfo` in `lstate.h`.
189	/// types.tsv: CallInfo → CallInfo (several fields renamed / adapted).
190	///
191	/// The C intrusive doubly-linked list (`previous`, `next` as raw pointers) is
192	/// replaced by `Option<CallInfoIdx>` indices into `LuaState::call_info`.
193	#[derive(Clone)]
194	pub struct CallInfo {
195	    // C: StkIdRel func — stack index of the called function value
196	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.func → StackIdx
197	    pub func: StackIdx,
198	
199	    // C: StkIdRel top — stack-top reservation for this call

delta.text: `get_at` does:** Reads the `LuaValue` at the given stack slot, mirroring the established
`set_at` pattern. `idx` is converted via `StackIdxConv` to a raw `StackIdx`, then used to
index `self.stack: Vec<StackValue>`. Out-of-bounds slots return `LuaValue::Nil` rather than panicking —
this matches C `index2value`'s graceful fallback to `G(L)->nilvalue` for indices past

side:** None — uses only `Vec::get`, `Clone`, and existing field accessors.

content.text:
Build is green.

**File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1063-1068`

**What `get_at` does:** Reads the `LuaValue` at the given stack slot, mirroring the established `set_at` pattern. `idx` is converted via `StackIdxConv` to a raw `StackIdx`, then used to index `self.stack: Vec<StackValue>`. Out-of-bounds slots return `LuaValue::Nil` rather than panicking — this matches C `index2value`'s graceful fallback to `G(L)->nilvalue` for indices past `top` (lapi.c:65).

**`todo!()` calls inside:** None — uses only `Vec::get`, `Clone`, and existing field accessors.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` green (warnings only).
